question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onPublish() in RestSubscriber] Can an unprivileged external user cause the RestSubscription sink.tryEmitNext() calls in onPublish() to fail (FAIL_NON_SERIALIZED or FAIL_OVERFLOW) by flooding the sink buffer, causing published transactions to be dropped from REST verification and masking gossip failures?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe() in RestSubscriber] Can an unprivileged external user cause the REST API to consistently return HTTP 404 for transaction lookups, triggering the shouldRetry() filter to retry up to maxAttempts times, exhausting the retry budget and causing the subscriber to silently drop transactions, masking the fact that gossiped transactions are not visible?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate() in PublishProperties] Can an unprivileged external user cause PublishProperties.validate() to throw IllegalArgumentException('There must be at least one publish scenario') by ensuring all scenarios are disabled or removed, preventing the application from starting and blocking all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTransactionResponse() sets node account IDs on the transaction object (transaction.setNodeAccountIds(node.getAccountIds())) only when request.getNode() is null, and if a stale node is reused across retries, cause the transaction to be directed to an offline node, preventing gossip?
